tasks.subject: [agent-hub-management-actions] [P1] Add deleteAgentData transport method
tasks.description:
Add a `deleteAgentData` method to the mesh transport methods factory.

**File:** `apps/client/src/layers/shared/lib/transport/mesh-methods.ts`

Add the following method inside the object returned by `createMeshMethods()`, in the `// --- Mesh Agent Discovery ---` section, after the existing `unregisterMeshAgent` method:

```typescript
deleteAgentData(id: string): Promise<{ success: boolean; deletedPath: string }> {
  return fetchJSON(baseUrl, `/mesh/agents/${id}/data`, { method: 'DELETE' });
},
```

This follows the same pattern as `unregisterMeshAgent` — uses `fetchJSON` with `DELETE` method, returns a typed Promise. The return type includes `deletedPath` which the server returns on success.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- Method is added to the object returned by `createMeshMethods()`
- Uses `fetchJSON(baseUrl, path, { method: 'DELETE' })`
- Returns `Promise<{ success: boolean; deletedPath: string }>`
- Matches the existing transport method pattern (no `async` keyword needed since `fetchJSON` returns a Promise)
tasks.activeForm: Adding deleteAgentData transport method to mesh-methods

tasks.subject: [agent-hub-management-actions] [P2] Create AgentManagementMenu component
[agent-hub-management-actions] [P2] Create DeleteAgentDialog component
tasks.description:
Create a type-to-confirm AlertDialog for irreversible `.dork` directory deletion.

**File:** `apps/client/src/layers/features/agent-hub/ui/DeleteAgentDialog.tsx`

Follow the pattern from the existing `UnregisterAgentDialog` in `apps/client/src/layers/features/agents-list/ui/UnregisterAgentDialog.tsx` but with a confirmation input.

```tsx
import { useState } from 'react';
import {
  AlertDialog,
  AlertDialogContent,
  AlertDialogHeader,
  AlertDialogFooter,
  AlertDialogTitle,
  AlertDialogDescription,
  AlertDialogAction,
  AlertDialogCancel,
} from '@/layers/shared/ui/alert-dialog';
import { Input } from '@/layers/shared/ui';
import { useDeleteAgentData } from '@/layers/entities/mesh';

interface DeleteAgentDialogProps {
  agentId: string;
  agentName: string;
  projectPath: string;
  open: boolean;
  onOpenChange: (open: boolean) => void;
}
```

**Dialog content:**
- **Title:** `Delete {agentName} & Data`
- **Description:** "This will permanently delete the `.dork` directory at `{projectPath}/.dork/`, including:" followed by a list:
  - `agent.json` -- agent manifest
  - `SOUL.md` -- personality convention
  - `NOPE.md` -- restriction convention
  - Any other convention files
- **Confirmation input:** Label: "Type **{agentName}** to confirm". Standard `<Input>` with `data-testid="delete-confirm-input"`.
- **Actions:**
  - Cancel button (outline style, default AlertDialogCancel)
  - "Delete Agent & Data" button with destructive styling: `className="bg-destructive text-destructive-foreground hover:bg-destructive/90"`, disabled until input value matches `agentName` exactly (case-sensitive)

**On confirm:**
1. Call `useDeleteAgentData().mutate(agentId)`
2. On success: close dialog via `onOpenChange(false)`, show `toast.error('Deleted {agentName} and all data')` (destructive toast)
3. The parent component handles navigation away from the deleted agent

**State management:** Use local `useState` for the confirmation input value. Reset to empty string when dialog opens/closes.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- Renders agent name and path info in the dialog
- Delete button is disabled until confirmation input matches agent name exactly
- Calls `useDeleteAgentData` mutation on confirm
- Closes dialog on success
- Shows destructive toast on success
- Input resets when dialog opens/closes

tasks.subject: [agent-hub-management-actions] [P2] Integrate AgentManagementMenu into AgentHubHero
tasks.description:
Add the `AgentManagementMenu` overflow button to the AgentHub hero section and wire up the `DeleteAgentDialog`. **File:** `apps/client/src/layers/features/agent-hub/ui/AgentHubHero.tsx` **Changes:** 1. Import the new components: ```typescript import { AgentManagementMenu } from './AgentManagementMenu'; import { DeleteAgentDialog } from './DeleteAgentDialog'; ``` 2. Add state for the delete dialog inside `AgentHubHero`: ```typescript const [deleteDialogOpen, setDeleteDialogOpen] = useState(false); ``` 3. Add the `AgentManagementMenu` to the hero JSX. Position it at the top-right corner of the hero section. Place it inside the `motion.div` with `data-slot="agent-hub-hero"`, after the shared panel header div but positioned absolutely: ```tsx {/* Management menu — top-right corner */}
setDeleteDialogOpen(true)} />
``` The `top-12` offset accounts for the `RightPanelHeader` height. Adjust as needed so the button sits visually at the top-right of the hero content area, below the panel header tabs. 4. Add the `DeleteAgentDialog` at the bottom of the component return, after the spacing div: ```tsx ``` Note: `useAgentHubContext()` already provides `projectPath` (see `AgentHubContextValue` interface). You'll need to destructure it from the context: ```typescript const { agent, onUpdate, previewColor, isPickerOpen, projectPath } = useAgentHubContext(); ``` 5. The `AgentHubHero` props interface does NOT need to change -- the menu is self-contained. **Acceptance criteria:** - `AgentManagementMenu` trigger (three-dot button) appears at top-right of hero section - Button is positioned below the `RightPanelHeader` tabs/close button - Clicking "Delete Agent & Data" in the menu opens `DeleteAgentDialog` - `DeleteAgentDialog` receives correct `agentId`, `agentName`, and `projectPath` - The hero's existing layout, animations, and interactions are unaffected

tasks.subject: [agent-hub-management-actions] [P3] Wire onManage callback in AgentsList and remove unused handlers
tasks.description:
Update `AgentsList` to wire the new `onManage` callback and remove the now-unused handlers and dialog.

**File:** `apps/client/src/layers/features/agents-list/ui/AgentsList.tsx`

**Step 1:** Remove unused imports and state:
- Remove `getAgentDisplayName` from `@/layers/shared/lib` import (if only used for unregister)
- Remove `UnregisterAgentDialog` import
- Remove `unregisterTarget` state (`useState<{ id: string; name: string } | null>(null)`)
- Remove `handleSetDefault` callback
- Remove `handleUnregister` callback
- Remove the `queryClient` variable if only used by `handleSetDefault` (check if still needed)
- Remove the `transport` variable if only used by `handleSetDefault` (check -- it may be used by the config query)

**Step 2:** Add a `handleManage` callback that opens the AgentHub panel:

```typescript
const handleManage = useCallback(
  (projectPath: string) => {
    useAgentHubStore.getState().openHub(projectPath);
    setActiveRightPanelTab('agent-hub');
    setRightPanelOpen(true);
  },
  [setActiveRightPanelTab, setRightPanelOpen]
);
```

Note: This is essentially the same as the existing `handleEdit` callback. Replace `handleEdit` with `handleManage` (same logic, better name to match the new callback).

**Step 3:** Update the `createAgentColumns` call to pass the new callback interface:

```typescript
const columns = useMemo(
  () =>
    createAgentColumns({
      onNavigate: handleNavigate,
      onManage: handleManage,
      onStartSession: handleStartSession,
    }),
  [handleNavigate, handleManage, handleStartSession]
);
```

**Step 4:** Remove the `UnregisterAgentDialog` JSX at the bottom of the component return (the `{unregisterTarget && ...}` block).

**Step 5:** Keep the `transport` and `queryClient` variables if they are used by the config query (`useQuery({ queryKey: ['config'], ... })`). Remove only if truly unused after cleanup.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- `AgentsList` passes `onNavigate`, `onManage`, `onStartSession` to `createAgentColumns`
- `handleManage` opens AgentHub panel via `useAgentHubStore.getState().openHub()` and `setRightPanelOpen(true)`
- `UnregisterAgentDialog` is no longer rendered in this component
- `handleSetDefault`, `handleUnregister`, `handleEdit` callbacks are removed (replaced by `handleManage`)
- `unregisterTarget` state is removed
- No unused imports remain
- Component still renders correctly with filter bar, data table, loading state, and empty state
tasks.activeForm: Wiring onManage callback in AgentsList and cleaning up unused handlers

tasks.subject: [agent-hub-management-actions] [P4] Update agent-columns tests for new split button layout
tasks.description:
Update the existing agent-columns tests to reflect the new split Chat + Manage buttons replacing the old Edit + overflow menu pattern.

First check if there are existing tests at `apps/client/src/layers/features/agents-list/__tests__/agent-columns.test.tsx` or similar. If not, the relevant tests may be in `AgentsList.test.tsx` or `AgentRow.test.tsx`.

**Changes needed:**

1. Update any tests that reference the old `AgentColumnCallbacks` interface to use the new 3-callback interface:
```typescript
const callbacks: AgentColumnCallbacks = {
  onNavigate: vi.fn(),
  onManage: vi.fn(),
  onStartSession: vi.fn(),
};
```

2. Remove tests for `onEdit`, `onSetDefault`, `onUnregister` callbacks if they existed.

3. Update tests that verify the Actions column rendering:
- Instead of checking for Edit button + overflow menu, verify two icon buttons exist
- Check that Chat button has correct `aria-label` (e.g., `Chat with {agentName}`)
- Check that Manage button has correct `aria-label` (e.g., `Manage {agentName}`)
- Verify clicking Chat calls `onStartSession` with correct project path
- Verify clicking Manage calls `onManage` with correct project path

4. In `apps/client/src/layers/features/agents-list/__tests__/AgentsList.test.tsx`, update:
- Remove any `UnregisterAgentDialog` related assertions
- Update the `createAgentColumns` mock or the actual callback wiring to match the new interface
- Remove references to `handleSetDefault`, `handleUnregister`, `handleEdit`

5. In `apps/client/src/layers/features/agents-list/__tests__/AgentRow.test.tsx` (if it exists and tests the actions column), update similarly.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- All existing tests in the agents-list feature still pass after modifications
- Tests verify the new Chat and Manage buttons are rendered
- Tests verify correct callbacks are invoked with correct arguments
- No tests reference removed callbacks (`onEdit`, `onSetDefault`, `onUnregister`)
- No tests reference `UnregisterAgentDialog` (it was removed from AgentsList)
tasks.activeForm: Updating agent-columns and AgentsList tests for new button layout
